threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You say 'thank you' to bus drivers. Just wait, you'll stop once you see the rolling eye of a local.
Last night on the 35, a guy serenaded the whole bus with classic soul. Everyone joined in for a verse; who knew Brixton had such talent at midnight?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I heard a group of kids arguing over who could hold the biggest slice of döner while an old lady fed pigeons. That's Neukölln life.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain does the sound of techno spill from a hidden bunker on a Sunday morning, while families picnic in nearby parks. It's a surreal combo.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Locals know to avoid Civic Center during BART meltdowns—especially when there's a dog on the tracks. Tourists just stand confused.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy casually juggling while waiting for the bus at 6th and Market. Only in SF do you get street performers and transit delays as your daily norm.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the bohemian vibe, but it’s the locals queuing at the bakeries that keep the real heartbeat alive.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them easily; they’re the ones still trying to navigate the metro with that old paper map, while everyone else swipes their T10 pass.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look puzzled when you see someone ordering a flat white with oat milk and talking about their vinyl collection.
Caught a night bus to Brixton and overheard a guy explain how he lost his job at a bakery because he kept eating the doughnuts. Classic.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The way the bakery on Hermannstrasse runs out of the best bread by noon and everyone still lines up like it’s gold, that’s when I knew.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin will you find a club in a former bakery, where the smell of fresh bread mixes with pounding beats at 2 AM. Truly unique!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You see someone eyeing a burrito at La Taqueria like it’s a long-lost friend, while others just order and go. That's a true local vibe.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 14 during rush hour, watched a guy juggle while singing 'California Love.' Everyone seemed more entertained than annoyed by the delays.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists parade along Carrer de Verdi, but few notice the hidden plazas where locals sip vermut, missing the real Gràcia vibe completely.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by their confused faces at the Mercat de la Llibertat, wondering why there’s no cheese in the jamón section.
